behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: taxonomy config/request-builder/API-client wiring can drop labels even if each function is correct in isolation (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this; a stub transport that captures outbound payload is sufficient (REAL-INFRA no). Correctness does not depend on model output content (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System/Agentic because behavior only validates outbound payload composition, not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API transport and assert captured outbound request contains exactly the full taxonomy label set for multiple taxonomies; fail if any label is missing or reordered/serialized incorrectly per contract.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization/validation logic on returned label text; real failure can be caught within one function/object boundary with no I/O. No module-to-module interaction is required by the behavior text itself. Rejected Integration because no cross-module state handoff is necessary to reproduce invalid-label mapping failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/label-validation logic with out-of-taxonomy labels (case variants, whitespace, arbitrary strings) and assert output is always "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets determinism of normalization step for identical input; this is pure local logic with no I/O. Rejected higher levels because no external dependency or cross-module orchestration is needed to expose nondeterministic normalization bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly with the exact same raw response payload and assert identical final label each run; include randomized repetition count to detect hidden mutable-state effects.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires repeated live model calls and checks consistency of returned classification. Correctness depends on real model behavior/non-determinism and provider response variation (LLM-DEP yes). Mocked/stubbed responses would hide this class of failure (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live calls (same document/taxonomy/prompt settings), record provider raw responses and final labels, and assert label stability conditioned on unchanged provider response; flag drift cases where label changes without response change.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at handoff between token-budget policy, request construction, and dispatch gate (cross-module orchestration). Can be caught with in-process tokenizer/policy wiring plus fake API client; no real network/OS required (REAL-INFRA no). Rejected System because behavior is explicitly pre-dispatch enforcement.
behaviors.test_description: Configure tight budgets and exercise documents around boundary sizes; assert module truncates/rejects per policy and that fake API client is only called when post-policy request is within budget.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real provider-reported usage accounting from a real API call (REAL-INFRA yes: real HTTP/external API). A mock would trivially pass and hide mismatches between local estimator and provider billing/tokenization (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Agentic: behavior checks token-usage infrastructure, not model decision quality/content.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live call(s) with representative document and parse provider usage metadata; assert reported prompt/completion/total tokens do not exceed configured budget threshold for the call policy.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is retry orchestration across API adapter, backoff policy, and caller-facing error handling (module interaction). This can be reproduced with a deterministic fake API sequence (rate-limit then success) and fake clock; real OS/network not required. Rejected System because behavior text is protocol handling logic, not real network-condition fidelity.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake API that returns rate-limit signal then success; assert module performs wait/backoff and retry attempts, eventually returns success, and does not surface immediate error to caller.
